methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $5.4B

data.narrative:
Climeworks, a Series F climate technology company founded by Christoph Gebald and Jan Wurzbacher, has raised over $1.0B to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies—TAE Technologies, Helion, Form Energy, and Watershed—alongside broader Climate & Energy comparables that include one public company (Lnza) and eight additional private peers including Commonwealth Fusion Systems and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($1.0B) as a proxy base value and applying a typical 1.3x Series F step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $1.3B. Peer-median valuation, drawn from the four direct comparables, yields $4.5B, with a range spanning $1.8B (Watershed) to $6.0B (TAE Technologies). The peer median reflects meaningful dispersion across the sector at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.1B to $5.1B, with a median of $2.9B. This wide range reflects two material uncertainties: (1) substantial valuation divergence among peer companies in Climate & Energy at Series F/G, suggesting heterogeneous risk profiles or growth expectations, and (2) the stage-progression method's reliance on total raised as a proxy rather than explicit company financials. The peer-median approach carries more weight given the availability of four direct comparables, though the resulting $4.5B estimate remains sensitive to peer selection and market conditions in the capital-intensive climate tech sector.
